tasks.title: Fix quest objective completion not being marked in UI
tasks.description: As a player, when I complete a quest objective (reading passage, writing task, listening, talking to NPC, answering questions), I need it to be visibly marked as completed in both the Quests page and the Objectives HUD. Core bug: objectives are being completed in the engine but the UI isn't reflecting it — likely a field name mismatch ('completed' vs 'isCompleted') or missing event subscription. QuestIndicatorManager also has separate completion logic that may conflict. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/QuestCompletionEngine.ts, trace the completeObjective() flow (~line 324) and verify it correctly sets objective.completed = true AND fires onObjectiveCompleted callback. (2) In client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonGame.ts, verify the onObjectiveCompleted callback is registered and updates the quest tracker UI (BabylonQuestTracker). (3) In the Quests tab (client/src/components/QuestsTab.tsx), verify that the objective checklist reads from the correct field — check for both 'completed' and 'isCompleted' field name inconsistency. (4) In QuestIndicatorManager.ts (~lines 96-122), fix the completion check to delegate to QuestCompletionEngine rather than reimplementing with separate logic that checks objectives.every(o => o.isCompleted || o.completed). (5) Add a unified objective completion status getter that all UI components use: QuestCompletionEngine.isObjectiveComplete(questId, objectiveId). (6) For the Objectives HUD overlay in the game view, ensure it subscribes to objective completion events and updates checkmarks in real-time. (7) Test with the Arrival Assessment quest: complete all 5 phases and verify each objective gets checked off. (8) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Wire Arrival Assessment phase completion to objective tracking
tasks.description: As a player completing the Arrival Assessment quest, I need each assessment phase (reading, writing, listening, initiate_conversation, conversation) to automatically mark its corresponding quest objective as completed when the phase finishes. Assessment phases fire events but those events don't reach QuestCompletionEngine. The bridge exists server-side but the client-side event-to-objective wiring is missing. (1) In server/services/assessment-quest-bridge.ts, verify that markPhaseCompleted() (~line 166) correctly updates the quest objective's completed flag and persists it to the database. (2) In client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonGame.ts, verify the assessment_phase_completed event (~line 2852) is caught by GameEventBus and routed to QuestCompletionEngine. (3) Add explicit mapping: when assessment_phase_completed fires with phase 'arrival_reading', find the Arrival Assessment quest and call completeObjective() for the reading objective (objective index 0). (4) Same mapping for arrival_writing (index 1), arrival_listening (index 2), arrival_initiate_conversation (index 3), arrival_conversation (index 4). (5) After each phase completion, persist the updated quest state to the server via PUT /api/worlds/:worldId/quests/:questId or POST /api/quests/:questId/progress. (6) When all 5 phases complete, automatically mark the entire quest as completed and award the 50 XP reward. (7) Verify the Prolog predicate phase_score/3 is asserted after each phase so assessment_complete/1 can be evaluated. (8) Test: complete each assessment phase in order and verify objectives tick off sequentially. (9) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Fix quest acceptance — Accept Quest button must immediately create quest
tasks.description: As a player, when I press the Accept Quest button on an NPC's quest offer panel, the quest must be immediately added to my quest list without requiring a full conversation exchange. Currently: Accept opens chat, NPC must emit QUEST_ASSIGN marker, then POST creates quest. This is fragile — if player closes chat or NPC doesn't emit marker, quest is never created. Fix: Accept -> POST immediately -> then open chat. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonGame.ts (~lines 2425-2433), modify the questOfferPanel onResult handler so that when result === 'accepted', it immediately creates the quest via POST /api/worlds/:worldId/quests. (2) The quest creation payload should include: title, description, objectives, questType, difficulty, targetLanguage, assignedBy (NPC character ID), experienceReward — all taken from the offer data. (3) After successful quest creation, call the onQuestAssigned callback to add it to the active quest list, update quest tracker UI, and update quest indicators. (4) THEN open the chat with quest context (existing openChatWithQuestOffer flow) so the NPC can discuss the quest naturally — but the quest is already saved regardless of conversation outcome. (5) Remove the dependency on NPC AI emitting **QUEST_ASSIGN** marker for the initial quest assignment — that marker flow should only be used for quests discovered organically during conversation, not for panel-accepted quests. (6) Add error handling: if quest creation fails, show a toast error and don't open chat. (7) Test: press Accept Quest on 3 different NPCs, verify all 3 quests appear in Quests tab immediately. (8) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Fix quest offer panel layout — center text and fix spacing
tasks.description: As a player, I want the quest offer popup to look polished with evenly spaced, centered text instead of having excess empty space at the bottom. User reports 'a lot of space at the bottom of the popup' and wants centered, evenly spaced text. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/QuestOfferPanel.ts, audit the panel layout and identify the cause of excess bottom space. (2) Set the panel container to use vertical centering for its content stack. (3) Ensure quest title, description, objectives list, and rewards section are evenly distributed vertically. (4) Center-align the quest title text. (5) Center-align the Accept and Decline buttons horizontally with equal spacing. (6) If the panel height is fixed, reduce it to fit content snugly with consistent padding (20px top/bottom, 24px between sections). (7) If the panel height is dynamic, ensure it shrinks to fit content without leaving large empty gaps. (8) Test with quests of varying description lengths (short 1-liner, medium paragraph, long multi-paragraph). (9) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Fix quest marker proximity triggers — green portals must complete objectives
tasks.description: As a player, when I walk into a green quest marker/portal, it should trigger the associated objective completion (e.g., visiting the Notice Board location). User reports green markers appear but walking into them does nothing. The marker may be offset from the target, the radius too small, or the proximity check may not be running. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/QuestObjectManager.ts, verify checkLocationProximity() (~lines 1420-1437) is being called from the game loop in BabylonGame.ts (~line 8418). (2) Increase the default proximity radius from 5 to 8 units for visit_location objectives to make them easier to trigger. (3) Fix marker positioning: ensure the marker is placed AT the target location (e.g., the Notice Board mesh position), not offset from it — check if the y += 5 offset (line 954) is causing the marker to appear far above and visually misalign with the actual target. (4) Add a ground-level visual ring/circle (flat torus or disc mesh at Y=0.1) at the trigger radius so the player can see the activation zone clearly. (5) When proximity triggers, play a completion particle burst (green sparkles) and show a toast notification: 'Objective completed: [objective description]'. (6) Verify the visitLocation() handler (~lines 1442-1457) correctly calls completionEngine.completeObjective() and that the completion engine's callback chain fires. (7) Add a debug log when proximity check runs and when it triggers, to diagnose future issues. (8) Test with Arrival Assessment: verify walking to the Notice Board area completes the relevant visit_location objective. (9) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Fix camera/photography system freeze when taking a photo
tasks.description: As a player, when I press C to enter camera mode and Space to take a photo, the game should capture the image and return to normal — not freeze permanently. User reports the game permanently freezes when taking a photo. The screenshot API may be blocking the render loop. Also needs first-person camera switch for immersive photography. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonPhotographySystem.ts, trace the capturePhoto() method and identify the freeze point. (2) The likely culprit is Tools.CreateScreenshotUsingRenderTargetAsync() — this can freeze if the render target is too large or if there's a GPU sync issue. Add a try/catch wrapper around the entire capture flow. (3) Reduce screenshot resolution from 800x600 to 640x480 if performance is an issue, or use Tools.CreateScreenshotAsync() (simpler, less likely to freeze) as a fallback. (4) Add a timeout: if screenshot capture takes >3 seconds, abort and show an error toast instead of freezing. (5) After capture, ensure the viewfinder UI is restored (the code hides it before capture and should restore it after — verify this happens even on error). (6) Switch to first-person camera mode when entering photo mode (C key): disable third-person camera, attach camera to player head position (Y + 1.6m), enable mouse look for aiming. (7) When exiting photo mode (C again), restore the previous camera mode (third-person). (8) Verify captured photos appear in the Photo Book (P key) with proper labels and timestamps. (9) Test: take 5 photos in different locations without any freezes. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add Photo Library page to Game Menu with labeled photos
tasks.description: As a player, I want a Photo Library page in the Game Menu where I can view all my captured photos, see what's in each photo, and use photos to complete quests. BabylonPhotoBookPanel exists with grid view, labels, and favorites. But it may not render due to the photo capture freeze (US-006). Labels need NPC activity detection for quest integration. (1) Verify that BabylonPhotoBookPanel.ts is properly integrated into GameMenuSystem.ts as the 'photos' tab — the tab exists but may not be rendering correctly. (2) Each photo in the library grid should display: thumbnail, timestamp, location name, and auto-detected subject labels (NPCs, objects, buildings in frame). (3) Photo labels should identify: NPC names (if NPC mesh was in frame), building names, object types, and activities being performed by NPCs (e.g., 'Marie - cooking', 'Pierre - painting'). (4) Add a photo detail view: clicking a thumbnail shows full-size photo with all labels overlaid at their screen positions. (5) Add quest integration: if a quest objective requires 'take a photo of someone cooking', check captured photos' labels for matching NPC+activity combinations. (6) Add a 'Photo Quests' section that shows which photo-based objectives are active and what subjects are needed. (7) Photos should persist across save/load via the playthrough's GameSaveState. (8) Verify the P key opens Photo Book panel directly from gameplay (not just from menu). (9) Test: take photos of various NPCs and objects, verify labels are accurate. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add Clues page to Game Menu for main quest investigation
tasks.description: As a player investigating the disappearance of a local writer, I need a dedicated Clues page that catalogs all clues I've collected, organized by source and relevance. The Journal/Investigation Board exists with clue tracking fields but lacks a rich, interactive clue catalog. Texts already have a clueText field. This connects the main quest's investigation theme. (1) The Journal tab in GameMenuSystem.ts already has an Investigation Board — extend it with a dedicated 'Clues' sub-section or create a new 'clues' tab. (2) Each clue entry should show: clue text, source (NPC name, book title, location), discovery timestamp, and whether it's been 'followed up' on. (3) Clue categories: 'witness_testimony' (from NPC conversations), 'written_evidence' (from books/texts), 'physical_evidence' (from items/locations), 'photo_evidence' (from photos). (4) Add a clue discovery system: when player reads a text with a clueText field (from the texts schema), automatically add it to the Clues page. (5) When player completes a conversation that mentions the missing writer or related topics, extract clue keywords and add them as testimony clues. (6) When player photographs specific quest-relevant locations or items, add photo clues. (7) Show a connection visualization: clues that reference the same NPC, location, or topic should be visually linked (simple line connections or grouped). (8) Add a clue counter in the Journal tab header: 'Clues: 3/12 found'. (9) Emit a GameEventBus event 'clue_discovered' when a new clue is added, so quest objectives that require finding clues can be completed. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add conversational action detection for quest completion
tasks.description: As a player, when I perform specific conversational actions during NPC dialogue (asking about a topic, using vocabulary, discussing quest subjects), these should be detected and count toward quest objective completion. Conversation events (npc_talked, vocabulary_used, conversation_turn) already fire but are generic. Need specific conversational action types that map to quest objectives. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonChatPanel.ts, enhance processAssistantResponse() (~line 1550) to detect conversational action patterns beyond just vocabulary and grammar. (2) Add detection for these conversational action types: 'asked_about_topic' (player message contains quest-relevant keywords), 'used_target_language' (player wrote in French), 'answered_question' (player responded to NPC's question), 'requested_information' (player asked for directions/help), 'made_introduction' (player introduced themselves). (3) For each detected action, emit a specific event to GameEventBus: { type: 'conversational_action', action: 'asked_about_topic', topic: 'missing_writer', npcId: '...', questId: '...' }. (4) In QuestCompletionEngine.ts, add a handler for 'conversational_action' events that checks active quest objectives for matching completion criteria. (5) For assessment quest phases: 'arrival_reading' completes when player reads the passage (reading session ends), 'arrival_writing' completes when player submits written response, 'arrival_listening' completes when listening session ends, 'arrival_initiate_conversation' completes when player starts conversation with marked NPC, 'arrival_conversation' completes when player completes required conversation turns. (6) Add conversation turn counting per NPC: track how many meaningful exchanges the player has had (messages > 5 words in target language count as meaningful). (7) Create a ConversationalActionDetector class that encapsulates all pattern matching logic, keeping it separate from the chat panel. (8) Test: have a conversation about the missing writer, verify 'asked_about_topic' fires and relevant quest objective progresses. (9) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Catalog all available Quaternius animations and create action registry
tasks.description: As a developer, I need a complete registry of all character animations available from Quaternius models so we can map them to player/NPC actions. Currently 10 animation states defined in NPCAnimationController + idle variations. Quaternius models may have more animations embedded in the GLB files that aren't being used. Need full audit. (1) Scan all character model files in client/public/assets/models/characters/quaternius/ and extract every animation group name from each GLB/GLTF file. (2) Create shared/game-engine/animation-registry.ts that exports a complete ANIMATION_CATALOG: Record<string, { name, description, duration, loop, category }> for every unique animation found. (3) Categories: 'locomotion' (walk, run, sprint), 'social' (talk, listen, wave, greet), 'work' (hammer, chop, sweep, stir, knead, write, type, pour), 'leisure' (sit, eat, drink, sleep, fish, dance), 'combat' (attack, block, dodge), 'emote' (laugh, cry, cheer, point), 'activity' (mine, plant, harvest, craft, paint, read, pray). (4) For each animation, note which character models have it available (not all models share the same animation set). (5) Create a function getAvailableAnimations(modelId: string): string[] that returns animations for a given character model. (6) Create a function getModelsWithAnimation(animationName: string): string[] for reverse lookup. (7) Log a summary: total unique animations found, total per category, which models have the richest animation sets. (8) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add physical player action system with animation-based activities
tasks.description: As a player, I want to perform physical actions like fishing, mining, cooking, painting, and crafting that play character animations and produce game effects. Currently player can only talk, examine, read signs, enter buildings, and take photos. Adding physical actions dramatically increases gameplay content. Actions should map to Quaternius animations. (1) Create client/src/components/3DGame/PlayerActionSystem.ts that manages physical actions the player can perform at specific locations/objects. (2) Define action types based on available Quaternius animations (from animation registry): fishing (near water), mining (near rocks/mine), harvesting (near plants/garden), cooking (near kitchen/oven), crafting (near workbench), painting (near easel), reading (near bookshelf), praying (near altar/church), sweeping (near broom), chopping (near wood pile). (3) Each action requires: proximity to the right object/location, sufficient energy, and optionally a tool item in inventory. (4) When player initiates action: switch to action animation, show progress bar (3-10 seconds depending on action), on completion apply effects (item gained, skill XP, quest progress). (5) Add action hotspots to building interiors: kitchen has cooking spot, blacksmith has smithing spot, library has reading spot, church has prayer spot, farm has harvesting spot. (6) Show interaction prompt when near hotspot: '[G]: Cook at stove', '[G]: Mine ore', '[G]: Fish here'. (7) Each action produces appropriate items: fishing->fish, mining->ore/gems, harvesting->herbs/vegetables, cooking->prepared_food, crafting->tools/items. (8) Connect action completion to GameEventBus: emit 'physical_action_completed' with action type, location, items produced. (9) Wire to quest system: objectives like 'cook a meal' or 'mine 3 ore' can be completed via these actions. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add player action radial menu for contextual physical actions
tasks.description: As a player, I need a UI to select which physical action to perform when multiple options are available at a location. ActionRadialMenu.tsx exists for social/mental actions during dialogue. This extends it for physical world actions. (1) Create or enhance client/src/components/3DGame/actions/ActionRadialMenu.tsx to show available physical actions as a radial/wheel menu. (2) Trigger: long-press G or press a dedicated action key (e.g., Q) when near an action hotspot. (3) Menu shows: action name, icon (emoji or simple symbol), energy cost, estimated time, required tool (if any). (4) Gray out actions the player can't perform (insufficient energy, missing tool, on cooldown). (5) Selecting an action starts the action sequence (animation + progress bar from PlayerActionSystem). (6) Menu closes automatically when an action is selected or when player moves away from hotspot. (7) If only one action is available at a location, skip the menu and perform it directly on G press. (8) Menu supports both mouse click and keyboard number keys (1-8) for quick selection. (9) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add NPC activity animations visible to players for photo/observation quests
tasks.description: As a player, I want to see NPCs performing visible activity animations (painting, cooking, reading, sweeping) so I can photograph them or observe them for quest objectives. NPCAnimationController has work sub-animations (hammer, chop, etc.) that fall back to generic 'work'. Need to use business-specific animations and make them queryable for quest/photo systems. (1) In ScheduleExecutor.ts, when NPCs are at work inside buildings, assign them specific visible activity animations based on their business type (not just generic 'work'). (2) Activity animation mapping: Baker->knead_dough, Blacksmith->hammer, Restaurant worker->stir/cook, Tailor->work_sitting, Librarian->read, Bartender->pour, Farmer->harvest, Painter->paint, Sweeper->sweep, Writer->write. (3) NPCs performing activities should have a visible activity label floating above them: 'Cooking', 'Painting', 'Reading' etc. (small text, only visible within 15m). (4) When player photographs an NPC performing an activity, the photo label should include the activity: 'Marie - Cooking' (integrate with BabylonPhotographySystem label detection). (5) Add quest objective type 'photograph_activity': requires player to take a photo of an NPC performing a specific activity. (6) Add quest objective type 'observe_activity': requires player to watch (be within 10m for 5+ seconds) an NPC performing a specific activity. (7) NPCs should cycle between 2-3 activity animations while at work (e.g., cook: stir 30s -> idle 10s -> chop 20s -> idle 5s -> repeat). (8) Activity detection: expose a getCurrentActivity(npcId): string method on ScheduleExecutor for other systems to query. (9) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Fix item collection and connect it to quest objective completion
tasks.description: As a player, when I collect an item in the game world, it should be added to my inventory AND automatically complete any quest objective that requires that item. Item collection infrastructure exists (ExteriorItemManager, InteriorItemManager, BabylonInventory) but the chain from collection to quest completion may be broken. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/QuestObjectManager.ts, verify that the item collection flow (OnIntersectionEnterTrigger ~lines 880-892) correctly calls collectItem() and then trackCollectedItemByName(). (2) In QuestCompletionEngine.ts, verify trackCollectedItemByName() finds matching collect_item objectives and calls completeObjective(). (3) Add fallback matching: if objective says 'collect_item' with itemName='herbs' and player collects an item with name='Healing Herbs' or category='herbs', it should still match (case-insensitive, partial match). (4) After item collection, show a pickup notification: '[Item name] collected! (Quest: [quest name] - [objective])' if the item was quest-relevant. (5) For quantity-based objectives (collect 5 fish), increment the objective's current count and only complete when current >= required. (6) Persist collected items to inventory (BabylonInventory.addItem()) AND to server-side playthrough state. (7) If item has languageLearningData, show the target language word briefly on screen when collected. (8) Verify that quest items appear in inventory with the 'quest' type tag and can't be dropped while the quest is active. (9) Test: place 3 quest items in the world, collect them, verify inventory and quest progress both update. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Fix Library menu tab freeze and populate with collectible texts
tasks.description: As a player, I want the Library tab in the Game Menu to work without freezing and to show texts I've collected from bookshelves throughout the world. User reports Library causes game to freeze. The Library is a menu tab (not a building). The text system is fully built server-side with generation, schema, and API — just needs reliable client rendering and world integration. (1) Investigate and fix the Library tab (GameMenuSystem.ts 'notices' tab ~lines 4698-5160) freeze — profile the render to find the bottleneck (likely too many articles rendering at once or texture loading). (2) Add pagination or virtual scrolling: only render 10-20 articles at a time, load more on scroll. (3) If freeze is caused by notice generation (NoticeGenerator.ts creating content synchronously), make generation async and show loading spinner. (4) Populate Library with texts from the server: on menu open, fetch GET /api/worlds/:worldId/texts and merge with local notices. (5) Show texts organized by documentType tabs: Books, Stories, Poems, Journals, Letters, Recipes, Notices. (6) Each text entry shows: title (in target language), author, difficulty/CEFR level, page count, read/unread status. (7) Texts with clueText should show a magnifying glass icon indicating they contain a clue. (8) Track reading progress: which texts the player has read and which comprehension questions they've answered correctly. (9) Award XP for completing comprehension questions (readingXp field from article schema). (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Place collectible books on bookshelves throughout the game world
tasks.description: As a player, I want to find books on bookshelves in libraries, homes, and shops that I can collect and read in my Library. Text system exists server-side with spawnLocationHint but no code actually spawns physical book meshes in the world. This bridges the gap. (1) In InteriorItemManager.ts, add a 'book' item type that can be placed on bookshelf furniture meshes. (2) When a building interior has a bookshelf, spawn 1-3 book items on the shelf (small box meshes with colored spine, ~0.3x0.2x0.05m). (3) Each book links to a text from the server's texts collection via textId — use the text's spawnLocationHint to place books appropriately (library->library building, residence->home bookshelves, hidden->secret rooms). (4) Book interaction: player presses G near a book -> show a pickup prompt '[G]: Pick up [book title]' -> add to inventory AND unlock in Library tab. (5) Books in inventory show as 'quest' or 'collectible' type items with a book icon. (6) Reading a book from inventory or Library opens the multi-page reading view with vocabulary highlights and comprehension questions. (7) Generate book distribution: use text-seed-generator.ts templates to seed 12+ books per world, then distribute them across buildings based on spawnLocationHint. (8) Main quest books (by the missing writer) should be placed in key locations: the writer's home, the library, a hidden cache, NPCs' homes. (9) Ensure book spawn positions are deterministic (seeded) so they're the same across sessions. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add Texts section to the Insimul Editor for managing reading content
tasks.description: As a world builder, I need a Texts section in the Insimul Editor where I can create, edit, and manage reading content (books, journals, letters) that gets placed in the game world. AdminTextsHub.tsx exists (639 lines) but may not be wired into the admin navigation. The API endpoints exist. This ensures world builders can manage text content. (1) Verify that client/src/components/admin/AdminTextsHub.tsx exists and is accessible from the admin panel navigation. (2) If AdminTextsHub isn't in the navigation, add it as a new tab: 'Texts' with a book icon, under the Content section of the admin sidebar. (3) The Texts editor should list all texts for the current world with: title, documentType, difficulty, CEFR level, author, status (draft/published), page count. (4) Add create/edit form with fields: title, titleTranslation, documentType dropdown (book/journal/letter/flyer/recipe), pages (multi-page editor with content + translation per page), vocabularyHighlights, comprehensionQuestions, cefrLevel, difficulty, authorName, clueText (optional), spawnLocationHint dropdown, tags. (5) Add a 'Generate' button that calls POST /api/worlds/:worldId/texts/generate to create a new text via LLM with configurable parameters: topic, documentType, difficulty, page count, whether to include a main quest clue. (6) Add a 'Seed Templates' button that calls POST /api/worlds/:worldId/texts/seed to populate with the 12+ built-in book templates. (7) Add bulk actions: generate 5 texts at once, publish all drafts, delete selected. (8) Show preview: clicking a text shows the reading view as it would appear in-game. (9) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Generate French reading texts connected to the main quest and world lore
tasks.description: As a player in a French-learning game, I need the collectible texts to be written in French at appropriate difficulty levels, with some texts containing clues about the missing writer. text-seed-generator.ts has 12+ templates but they need expansion. The main quest is about a local writer who disappeared — their writings should be collectible clues. (1) Extend text-seed-generator.ts to include 20+ template texts in French covering multiple genres: fiction (short stories), non-fiction (local history), journal entries (the missing writer's diary), letters (correspondence between characters), recipes (local cuisine), poems. (2) All texts must be in French with English translations provided in contentTranslation fields. (3) Difficulty distribution: 8 texts at A1 (beginner), 6 at A2, 4 at B1, 2 at B2. (4) Main quest integration: 5 texts should be written by the missing writer character, containing progressive clues about their disappearance (each text reveals a new piece of the mystery). (5) Each text includes: 3-5 vocabulary highlights with translations, 2-3 comprehension questions per page. (6) Text topics should relate to the in-game world: local history, settlement events, character backstories, regional culture (Louisiana Creole/Chitimacha connections). (7) Texts by the missing writer should have a distinctive style and reference specific locations/characters in the settlement. (8) Add a generation prompt template for LLM-based text creation that enforces: target language, CEFR constraints, clue embedding, world-contextual content. (9) Run seed generation for a test world and verify all 20+ texts are created with proper French content. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add reading quests that require reading a text and answering questions
tasks.description: As a player, I want quests that require me to find and read a specific book, then answer comprehension questions about it to prove I understood the text. Library tab already has comprehension questions and XP. This formalizes reading into quest objectives and connects it to the quest system. (1) Create a ReadingQuestGenerator in server/services/ that generates reading quests from available texts. (2) Each reading quest has objectives: 1) Find the book (collect_item), 2) Read the text (complete_reading), 3) Answer comprehension questions (answer_questions). (3) Add quest objective type 'complete_reading': completed when player reads all pages of a specific text (tracked by textId). (4) Add quest objective type 'answer_questions': completed when player answers N out of M comprehension questions correctly (e.g., 2/3 correct). (5) In the Library reading view, after finishing a text's comprehension questions, emit 'reading_completed' and 'questions_answered' events to GameEventBus with { textId, score, questionsCorrect, questionsTotal }. (6) Wire these events to QuestCompletionEngine for objective completion. (7) Generate 5-8 reading quests per world, one per major text, with XP rewards scaled by difficulty (A1: 20 XP, A2: 35 XP, B1: 50 XP, B2: 75 XP). (8) NPCs who are teachers, librarians, or scholars should offer reading quests: 'Have you read [book title]? I'd love to discuss it with you.' (9) Completing a reading quest about a main-quest text should also add the embedded clue to the Clues page. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Connect all main quest tasks to completable in-game actions
tasks.description: As a player, every task in the main quest (and the Arrival Assessment) must be connected to a specific in-game action that I can actually perform to complete it. The 5 Arrival Assessment objectives are currently not being marked complete because the assessment phase events don't connect to objective completion. This is the most critical fix for playability. (1) Audit all main quest objectives and map each to a specific game trigger. (2) 'Read a short passage' -> trigger: player opens reading view for the assigned text and reads all pages -> emit 'reading_completed' event -> complete objective. (3) 'Complete writing tasks' -> trigger: player submits a written response in the assessment writing panel (minimum 20 words in French) -> emit 'writing_submitted' event -> complete objective. (4) 'Listen to a passage in French' -> trigger: player plays the audio in the listening assessment and answers questions -> emit 'listening_completed' event -> complete objective. (5) 'Talk to the marked NPC' -> trigger: player initiates conversation with the marked NPC (npc_talked event) -> complete objective. (6) 'Answer the NPC's questions' -> trigger: player completes 3+ conversation turns with the assessment NPC -> emit 'conversation_assessment_completed' -> complete objective. (7) For each trigger, create or verify the event emission point in the relevant UI component (reading panel, writing panel, listening panel, chat panel). (8) For each trigger, create or verify the QuestCompletionEngine handler that matches the event to the objective. (9) Add explicit event-to-objective mapping configuration so new quests can declaratively specify their completion triggers. (10) Test end-to-end: start Arrival Assessment, complete all 5 objectives via their triggers, verify quest completes and XP is awarded. (11) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Ensure all player actions have Insimul action definitions with preconditions and effects
tasks.description: As a developer, every player action in the game must be backed by an Insimul Action record with requires (preconditions) and effects (postconditions) defined in Prolog content. Actions exist in schema with Prolog content support, but many player actions may not have corresponding database records. Need complete coverage. (1) Audit all player actions (talk, examine, read_sign, enter_building, take_photo, collect_item, fish, mine, harvest, cook, craft, pray, sweep, read_book, answer_question, accept_quest, attack) and ensure each has a corresponding Action record in the database. (2) For each action, define Prolog content with: requires() clauses (preconditions: energy, proximity, tool, skill level) and effect() clauses (postconditions: item gained, XP, skill progress, relationship change, quest progress). (3) Create a seed migration or generation step that populates these action definitions for every world. (4) Example action definitions: fishing requires(near(water), has(fishing_rod), energy_gte(15)), effects: item_add(fish, 1), skill_xp(fishing, 10), energy(-15). cooking requires(near(stove), has(ingredients), energy_gte(10)), effects: item_add(prepared_meal, 1), skill_xp(cooking, 15), energy(-10). (5) In ActionManager.ts, verify canPerformAction() checks the Prolog preconditions before allowing the action. (6) In ActionManager.ts, verify performAction() applies all Prolog effects after successful completion. (7) Actions should be queryable: given a quest objective ('cook a meal'), the system can find which action satisfies it. (8) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add fishing activity with animation, items, and quest integration
tasks.description: As a player, I want to fish near water to catch fish, gain XP, and complete fishing-related quests. Water bodies exist in the terrain system. Fishing is a natural activity for a settlement game and provides vocabulary learning through item names. (1) Add fishing hotspot detection: when player is within 5m of a water body (river, lake, pond — detect via terrain/mesh type), show prompt '[G]: Fish here'. (2) Fishing action sequence: cast animation (if available, else wave) -> waiting idle (5-15 seconds with random catch timer) -> catch animation (if available, else quick pull gesture) -> item awarded. (3) Fish types: common_fish (60% chance), rare_fish (25%), legendary_fish (10%), nothing (5%). (4) Each fish is an inventory item with: name in French (e.g., 'Poisson', 'Truite', 'Saumon'), English translation, languageLearningData for vocabulary. (5) Show catch notification: 'You caught a [fish name in French]! ([English translation])' with brief vocabulary popup. (6) Energy cost: 15 per cast. Fishing rod tool optional (increases rare/legendary chance by 15%). (7) Add fishing quest objectives: 'Catch 3 fish', 'Catch a rare fish', 'Fish at the river' — completable via the fishing action events. (8) Emit 'physical_action_completed' event with { action: 'fishing', itemGained: fishType, location } for quest tracking. (9) Add a fishing skill that improves catch rates with practice. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add mining activity with animation, items, and quest integration
tasks.description: As a player, I want to mine rocks and ore deposits to collect minerals, gain XP, and complete mining quests. Mine building type exists in building-defaults.ts. Mining pairs naturally with blacksmith crafting. (1) Add mining hotspot: rocks/ore deposits near mountains or in mine buildings. Detect via mesh metadata or building type (Mine, Blacksmith backyard). (2) Mining action sequence: swing pickaxe animation (hammer animation as fallback) -> 3-5 hits with particle sparks -> ore/stone item awarded. (3) Mineral types: stone (50%), iron_ore (25%), copper_ore (15%), gem (8%), rare_gem (2%). (4) Each mineral has French/English names and languageLearningData (e.g., 'Pierre', 'Fer', 'Cuivre', 'Rubis'). (5) Energy cost: 20 per mining session. Pickaxe tool increases yield by 1 additional item. (6) Add mining quest objectives: 'Mine 5 iron ore', 'Find a gem', 'Mine at the quarry'. (7) Emit 'physical_action_completed' event for quest tracking. (8) Show mining result notification with French vocabulary. (9) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add plant collection and herbalism activity
tasks.description: As a player, I want to collect plants, herbs, and flowers from gardens and the wild to learn botanical vocabulary and complete herbalism quests. HerbShop building type exists. Plant collection is low-energy, vocabulary-rich, and fits the language-learning focus. (1) Add harvestable plant meshes in exterior areas: near farms, gardens, forest edges, and herb shop buildings. (2) Plant types: common_herb (40%), medicinal_herb (25%), flower (20%), rare_herb (10%), poisonous_plant (5%). (3) Harvesting action: player approaches plant -> '[G]: Pick [plant name]' -> brief pick animation -> item added to inventory. (4) All plant names in French with translations: 'Lavande' (Lavender), 'Romarin' (Rosemary), 'Menthe' (Mint), 'Rose', 'Thym' (Thyme), 'Sauge' (Sage), 'Basilic' (Basil). (5) Plants respawn after 1 game day (not immediately). (6) Energy cost: 5 per harvest (low, meant to be casual collection). (7) Add herbalism quests: 'Collect 3 different herbs', 'Find a rare medicinal herb', 'Deliver herbs to the herbalist NPC'. (8) Herbs can be used in cooking and alchemy (future crafting system). (9) Emit 'physical_action_completed' and 'item_collected' events for quest tracking. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add cooking/alchemy crafting activity
tasks.description: As a player, I want to combine collected ingredients (fish, herbs, minerals) at crafting stations to create items, learn recipe vocabulary, and complete crafting quests. Cooking, alchemy, and crafting naturally combine items from fishing, mining, and herbalism. Recipes are great vocabulary-learning vehicles. (1) Add crafting stations: kitchen stove (cooking), alchemy table (potions), and workbench (general crafting) in appropriate building interiors. (2) Cooking interaction: approach stove -> '[G]: Cook' -> opens simple crafting UI showing available recipes based on inventory ingredients. (3) Recipe system: each recipe has: name (French), ingredients list, result item, difficulty, XP reward. Example: 'Soupe de Poisson' requires fish + herbs -> prepared_soup. (4) Start with 8-10 recipes: fish soup, herb salad, bread (from bakery), healing potion (alchemy), stamina tonic, basic tool (workbench). (5) Crafting animation: stir/work animation plays during 3-5 second crafting time. (6) Result item added to inventory with French name and vocabulary data. (7) Crafting quests: 'Cook a fish soup', 'Brew a healing potion', 'Craft 3 different recipes'. (8) Show recipe vocabulary: when crafting, display ingredient names and result name in French with translations. (9) Emit 'craft_completed' event with { recipe, ingredients_used, result_item } for quest tracking. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add exploration and discovery activities
tasks.description: As a player, I want to discover hidden locations, investigate scenes, and find secret items to complete exploration quests and learn location vocabulary. Terrain features exist from the procgen system. This adds gameplay meaning to exploring the world and connects to the main quest mystery. (1) Add discoverable locations: hidden clearing, abandoned cabin, old well, cave entrance, ancient tree, secret garden — spawned in exterior world at terrain feature positions. (2) Discovery mechanic: when player enters a previously undiscovered location's radius (15m), show discovery notification: 'New location discovered: [French name] ([English])!'. (3) Each discovery adds the location to the Map tab with a custom icon and French/English label. (4) Add investigation points at discovered locations: clickable objects that reveal lore text, vocabulary words, or main quest clues. (5) Exploration quests: 'Discover 3 hidden locations', 'Investigate the abandoned cabin', 'Find the writer's secret spot'. (6) Main quest integration: the missing writer has 2-3 secret locations that contain critical clues about their disappearance. (7) Emit 'location_discovered' and 'investigation_completed' events for quest tracking. (8) Award exploration XP for each new discovery (10-25 XP based on location rarity). (9) Track discovery progress: 'Locations: 5/12 discovered' in the Map tab. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add quest chain progression for the main quest storyline
tasks.description: As a player, I want the main quest to progress through a chain of connected quests, each building on clues from the previous one, leading to the resolution of the missing writer mystery. The main quest concept (missing writer) is established but not formalized as a quest chain. This creates the full narrative arc that weaves through all game activities. (1) Define the main quest chain with 6-8 quests using questChainId and questChainOrder fields. (2) Chain 1: 'Arrival Assessment' (existing) — baseline language test, introduces the settlement. (3) Chain 2: 'The Notice Board' — read the missing person notice, talk to the town clerk, learn the writer's name. (4) Chain 3: 'The Writer's Home' — visit the writer's residence, collect their journal, read first clue. (5) Chain 4: 'Following the Trail' — talk to 3 NPCs who knew the writer, collect witness testimonies as clues. (6) Chain 5: 'The Hidden Writings' — find 3 of the writer's books in the world, read them for embedded clues. (7) Chain 6: 'The Secret Location' — follow clues to discover the writer's secret spot, investigate the scene. (8) Chain 7: 'The Final Chapter' — confront the truth about the writer's disappearance through a conversation challenge. (9) Chain 8: 'Departure Assessment' (existing) — final language test measuring progress. (10) Each quest unlocks automatically when the previous one completes (via questChainId ordering). (11) Quest completion should feel natural: each quest requires different action types (reading, conversation, exploration, photography, item collection). (12) Create the quest records in a migration or seed script so they exist for every new world. (13) Verify QuestCompletionManager.ts handles chain progression: completing quest N activates quest N+1. (14) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Create action-to-quest mapping system for declarative quest design
tasks.description: As a developer, I need a system where quest objectives declaratively specify which game actions complete them, so new quests can be created without writing custom completion code. Currently QuestCompletionEngine has hardcoded handlers per objective type. A declarative system makes it easy to add new quest types and ensures consistency. (1) Create shared/game-engine/quest-action-mapping.ts that defines a mapping between quest objective types and game event types. (2) Mapping structure: { objectiveType: 'collect_item', eventType: 'item_collected', matchFields: { itemName: 'objective.targetItem' } }. (3) Support all objective types: collect_item->item_collected, visit_location->location_visited, talk_to_npc->npc_talked, complete_reading->reading_completed, answer_questions->questions_answered, photograph_activity->photo_taken, physical_action->physical_action_completed, craft_item->craft_completed, discover_location->location_discovered. (4) In QuestCompletionEngine.ts, replace hardcoded objective type switch statements with a generic event matcher that uses the mapping. (5) The matcher should: receive a GameEvent, look up which objective types can be satisfied by that event type, check if any active objective matches the event's fields, and complete matching objectives. (6) Support compound objectives: 'photograph someone cooking' requires both photo_taken AND subject_activity=cooking in the same event. (7) Support quantity objectives: 'collect 5 fish' increments current count on each matching event. (8) Quest designers can create new objectives by choosing from the event catalog without touching completion code. (9) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add quest progress persistence to server
tasks.description: As a player, my quest objective progress (items collected, locations visited, conversations had) must persist when I save/load the game or refresh the browser. Quest progress may be lost on refresh if only tracked in client-side state. Needs reliable server persistence. (1) When a quest objective is completed, immediately save the updated quest state to the server via PUT /api/worlds/:worldId/quests/:questId. (2) Save the full objectives array with completed flags AND progress counters (current/required for quantity objectives). (3) When loading a game, merge saved quest state with world quest definitions: saved progress overwrites default state. (4) Verify PlaythroughQuestOverlay merges correctly: playthrough-scoped objectives override world-level defaults. (5) Save additional quest metadata: cluesFound, photosMatched, textsRead, conversationsCompleted — all in the quest's progress field. (6) On game load, restore quest markers for incomplete objectives (re-spawn markers at correct positions). (7) On game load, do NOT re-spawn markers for completed objectives. (8) Add auto-save: quest progress saves every 60 seconds in addition to on-change saves. (9) Test: complete 2 of 5 objectives, refresh browser, verify 2 remain completed and 3 remain pending with markers. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add NPC-assigned side quests with variety of action types
tasks.description: As a player, I want NPCs to offer diverse side quests that exercise different game mechanics — fetch quests, delivery quests, photo quests, reading quests, cooking quests, exploration quests. NPCs show quest available markers but the quests may be generic or non-functional. Procedural generation based on occupation makes quests feel contextual and exercises all game mechanics. (1) Create a SideQuestGenerator in server/services/ that procedurally generates side quests based on NPC occupation and personality. (2) Quest type templates by NPC occupation: Baker->'Bake a special bread' (cooking), Fisherman->'Catch rare fish' (fishing), Librarian->'Read and discuss a book' (reading), Guard->'Investigate a location' (exploration), Merchant->'Deliver goods to [NPC]' (delivery), Farmer->'Harvest herbs for medicine' (herbalism), Blacksmith->'Mine ore and forge a tool' (mining+crafting), Artist->'Photograph beautiful scenes' (photography). (3) Each generated quest has: 2-4 objectives, appropriate rewards (XP + items), difficulty matching player level, all text in French (with translations). (4) Generate 2-3 side quests per NPC with quest-giving capability (not every NPC gives quests). (5) Side quest objectives must use the declarative action mapping so they're automatically completable. (6) Quests should reference real in-game locations, items, and NPCs (not generic placeholders). (7) Rotate available quests: when player completes an NPC's quest, generate a new one after 1 game day. (8) Side quests contribute to the overall clue discovery if they involve relevant locations or NPCs. (9) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Fix container interaction — players must be able to open and loot containers
tasks.description: Players currently cannot open containers (chests, barrels, crates) in building interiors or the exterior world, even though a full container system exists (BabylonContainerPanel, ContainerManager, ContainerSpawnSystem). The interaction chain from clicking/pressing Enter on a container mesh to opening the container UI is broken. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonGame.ts, trace the interaction handler for container meshes — verify that when the player presses Enter or clicks on a mesh with metadata.isContainer === true, the code calls ContainerSpawnSystem.openContainerByMesh() and then BabylonContainerPanel.open(). (2) In InteriorItemManager.ts, verify that container furniture meshes (chests, barrels, crates) have metadata.isContainer = true set on the mesh so the interaction system can detect them. (3) In InteractionPromptSystem.ts, add container detection: when the player looks at a container mesh, show prompt '[Enter]: Open [container name]' — containers should be detected the same way objects and NPCs are (ray pick + proximity fallback). (4) Wire the Take button in BabylonContainerPanel (~lines 496-513) to call BabylonInventory.addItem() for the taken item AND emit an 'item_collected' event to GameEventBus with full item metadata. (5) Wire the Take All button (~lines 515-533) to do the same for every item in the container. (6) After taking an item from a container, call questObjectManager.trackCollectedItemByName(item.name) so quest objectives are checked. (7) Verify locked containers show the 'Locked' message and require the correct key item in inventory to open (ContainerManager.canUnlock checks keyItemId). (8) Verify container contents persist — reopening a looted container should show it empty (or respawned items if respawns=true and enough time passed). (9) Test: place a chest with 3 items in a building interior, open it, take items one by one, verify they appear in inventory and container shows as empty. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Fix shop/merchant purchasing — players must be able to buy items from shops
tasks.description: Players currently cannot purchase items from shops, even though BabylonShopPanel has a full buy/sell UI with language-learning integration. The shop panel may not be opening when interacting with merchant NPCs, or the merchant inventory may not be populated. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonGame.ts, verify that handleOpenShop(merchantNpcId) (~line 10319) is called when the player interacts with a merchant NPC inside a shop building. (2) If shops only open through NPC conversation, add a direct interaction path: when player presses G on a merchant NPC who is behind a counter (role='owner' or 'employee' in a commercial building), show an interaction choice: 'Talk' vs 'Trade' — selecting Trade opens the shop panel directly. (3) Verify merchant inventory generation: when a shop opens, the merchant must have items to sell. Check if ShopPanelConfig.items is populated from the business's stock or procedurally generated based on businessType. (4) If merchant items are empty, add a fallback: generate default stock based on business type — Bakery gets bread/pastries/flour, Blacksmith gets weapons/tools/armor, HerbShop gets herbs/potions, GroceryStore gets food/drinks, BookStore gets books, JewelryStore gets jewelry/gems, etc. (5) Verify player gold is tracked: check that the player has a gold amount stored in playthrough state or character data, and that BabylonShopPanel receives it on open. (6) If player has no gold system, initialize starting gold (e.g., 100 gold) and ensure gold earned from selling items or quest rewards persists. (7) After a buy transaction, verify item appears in player inventory, gold is deducted, and merchant stock decrements. (8) After a buy transaction, call questObjectManager.trackCollectedItemByName(item.name) so quest objectives are checked (already exists at line 10372 — verify it fires). (9) Test: enter a shop, verify merchant has items, buy an item, verify gold deducted and item in inventory. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add unified item collection hook that checks quest objective completion
tasks.description: Item collection from various sources (containers, shops, world pickups, NPC gifts) must all funnel through a single hook that checks whether the collected item fulfills any active quest objective. Currently trackCollectedItemByName() exists but is not called from all collection sources. (1) Create a centralized item collection handler in BabylonGame.ts: handleItemAcquired(item: InventoryItem, source: 'container' | 'shop' | 'world' | 'gift' | 'craft' | 'quest_reward') that is called from ALL item acquisition paths. (2) Inside handleItemAcquired: add item to inventory via BabylonInventory.addItem(), emit 'item_collected' to GameEventBus, call questObjectManager.trackCollectedItemByName(item.name), and show a pickup toast notification. (3) Update container Take handler (BabylonContainerPanel onTake callback) to call handleItemAcquired with source='container'. (4) Update shop Buy handler (handleShopBuy) to call handleItemAcquired with source='shop'. (5) Update world item pickup (ExteriorItemManager/InteriorItemManager collection callbacks) to call handleItemAcquired with source='world'. (6) Update any gift/reward flows to call handleItemAcquired with source='gift' or 'quest_reward'. (7) In QuestCompletionEngine.trackCollectedItemByName(), add fuzzy matching: if objective says 'herbs' and player collects 'Healing Herbs' or item.category='herbs', it should match (case-insensitive substring + category match). (8) For quantity objectives (collect 5 fish), verify the counter increments per collection and objective completes only when current >= required. (9) When a quest objective is completed by item collection, show a prominent notification: 'Quest objective completed: Collect [item]' with a checkmark animation. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Prevent items and natural elements from spawning on roads and streets
tasks.description: Items and natural elements (boulders, trees, bushes) are sometimes spawned in the middle of streets, which looks unrealistic. ExteriorItemManager has a position validator nudging system but it may not be properly configured with road zone data. ProceduralNatureGenerator has terrain-aware placement but no explicit road avoidance. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonGame.ts, when initializing ExteriorItemManager, verify that setPositionValidator() is called with a callback that returns true (blocked) for any position that falls on a road segment. (2) The road collision check should use the street network data (from RoadGenerator or the IR's street segments): for each candidate position, check if it's within roadHalfWidth + 1.5m of any road segment centerline. (3) If setPositionValidator is not being called, add it: pass a function that checks the position against all road segment bounding boxes or uses a rasterized road mask. (4) In ProceduralNatureGenerator.ts, add road avoidance to generateTerrainAwarePlacements(): before accepting a candidate position, check it against road segments. Reject positions within roadHalfWidth + 2m of any road centerline. (5) For boulders and rock features specifically (GeologicalFeatureType), add additional clearance: reject positions within 3m of road edges AND within 5m of building footprints. (6) Add sidewalk avoidance: also reject positions within 1.5m of sidewalk paths (sidewalk nodes from NPCScheduleSystem). (7) For already-spawned items/nature that overlap roads (in case of edge cases), add a post-spawn cleanup pass: after all items and nature are placed, iterate and remove any whose position overlaps a road zone. (8) Test: generate a settlement and visually verify no items, boulders, or trees appear on road surfaces. (9) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Populate containers with contextual loot and quest items
tasks.description: Containers need to be populated with items that make contextual sense for their location and occasionally include quest-relevant items so players are rewarded for exploring and looting. ContainerSpawnSystem has loot tables by building type but they may not be generating items or may need expansion. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/ContainerSpawnSystem.ts, verify that loot tables (~lines 59-106) are being used when containers are opened for the first time — check that openContainer() calls generateLoot() or equivalent. (2) Expand loot tables for all building types: add specific items for Bakery (fresh bread, pastry, flour sack), Library (old book, scroll, ink bottle), Church (holy water, candle, prayer beads), Farm (seeds, vegetables, milk jug), Tavern (ale mug, wine bottle, coin pouch), Blacksmith (iron ingot, horseshoe, dagger). (3) All loot item names should be in the target language (French) with English in the description field, and include languageLearningData so collecting them teaches vocabulary. (4) Add quest item injection: when generating loot for a container, check if any active quest has an unfound collect_item objective — if the item matches the building context, add it to one container in that building with 30% probability. (5) Add rare/special items: 5% chance of a container having a 'clue' item related to the main quest (a torn page, a cryptic note, etc.) that gets added to the Clues page when collected. (6) Ensure container contents are seeded deterministically (building ID + container index as seed) so the same container always has the same items across sessions until looted. (7) Test: open containers in 5 different building types and verify contents are contextually appropriate with French item names. (8) Typecheck passes.
